statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a string of comma-separated integers.
statement.outputFormat: Return true if the string can be represented as a valid queue (i.e., the elements are in a single queue in the order they are given), false otherwise.
statement.notes: A valid queue is defined as a sequence of elements where each element is either a number or a sequence of numbers separated by commas.

hints.text: A valid queue should only have one entry at the beginning and end.

editorial.title: Queue Formation
editorial.intuition: To form a queue from the given string, we need to parse the string and check if it meets the conditions of a valid queue.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute-force approach: Try all permutations of the input string and verify if any of them form a valid queue.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a stack to parse the string, and verify if the stack is a valid queue at the end.
commonMistakes: Not verifying if the stack is empty
